tools.pinNote: exact-ref install pin is a follow-up (needs an upstream tag/commit); pin recorded for drift-detection; unattended never installs.

macos: python3 -m pip install --user pipx || brew install pipx

tools.notes: FETCH role — unified internet-access CLI; auto-fallback backends; zero mandatory API fees. Returns raw content ingested as secondary blobs. Credentials local-only.

DISCOVERY role — recency-focused (last-30-days) multi-platform synthesizer. Use for LEADS ONLY: mine its cited sources / hot threads, then fetch those underlying sources as raw blobs and chase to primary. NEVER ingest its synthesized brief as a blob (charter Part 37: gatherers return raw material only). Free core (Reddit/HN/Polymarket/GitHub/X-via-cookies); optional keys (Brave/Perplexity/ScrapeCreators/etc). Install: '/plugin marketplace add mvanhorn/last30days-skill' or 'npx skills add mvanhorn/last30days-skill -g'. healthCmd is an OS-keyed probe resolved by web-reach-ensure, not an install-relative file path.

macos: python3 -m pip install --user pipx || brew install pipx

tools.invokeHint: crwl <url> -o markdown   # headless-browser crawl -> clean markdown; -o json|markdown-fit|all, -q <question>
tools.notes: FETCH role - LLM-friendly crawler that renders pages in a managed headless browser (Chromium via Playwright), so it reaches JavaScript / dynamic pages agent-reach's Jina-Reader path cannot. Returns clean markdown ingested as secondary blobs: confidence-capped, gated, and chased toward a primary like any open-web page. Licensed/inventoried sources (SemiAnalysis, TrendForce, ...) may be fetched with crwl and are flagged, not refused (D6); never use crwl to circumvent a paywall - only freely-served pages, never paid/subscriber-gated content. Install via pipx (isolated) + crawl4ai-setup (downloads Chromium); healthCmd is a fast console-script presence check that does not launch a browser.
